findings_addressed: G2 read.get/get_many (active-only superseded_at IS NULL, order-preserved, missing=None, get delegates to get_many)
G3 read.collection/mutations (mandatory limit clamped to ~1M cap + after-id cursor, ORDER BY id, read.mutations alias)
read.* namespace live in Py+TS (lockstep, identical arg shapes, single-source allowlist)
F2 + F4-READ subset delivered via the four governed read verbs
AC-074 governed surface: read.* now introspected-live (not vacuous); REQ-053 read.* surface bound by gap label + test names (no new ids)

tdd_evidence.red_proof: At 748dfca, `cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test pr_g2_get_by_id --no-run` failed to compile with E0599 (no method `read_get`/etc on Engine); the conformance flip referenced a non-existent `read` namespace. Conformance non-vacuity demonstrated post-GREEN: injecting a stray live `read.delete` into the TS `read` object made surface.test.ts FAIL with `live command read.delete is outside the governed allowlist` (then reverted).

reader_isolation: reads dispatch through ReaderWorkerPool DEFERRED-tx: new ReaderRequest::GetById -> read_get_by_id_in_tx (SELECT ... canonical_nodes WHERE logical_id IN (...) AND superseded_at IS NULL, BEGIN DEFERRED) and ReaderRequest::ReadCollection -> read_collection_in_tx (operational_mutations WHERE collection_name=? AND id>? ORDER BY id LIMIT ?clamped, BEGIN DEFERRED). Never self.connection.lock(). Each new variant carries its OWN typed respond channel (SyncSender<rusqlite::Result<Vec<Option<NodeRecord>>>> / Vec<OpStoreRow>>), so the Search ReaderResponse type alias and read_search_in_tx are byte-unchanged -> Slice 10 byte-identical-unfiltered Search pin (pr_g10_filtered_knn.rs::unfiltered_phase1_sql_byte_identical_to_0_7_2) green.
parity: read.get/get_many/collection/mutations identical signatures in Py (snake_case, read.py __all__) + TS (camelCase read object); allowlist single-source (src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json, 9 members unchanged); both surface suites introspect the live `read` namespace (Py read.__all__ loop; TS Object.keys(read) with camelCase->snake_case normalization) and assert the four read.* verbs are present-in-live (removal fails) + subset-of-allowlist; recovery suites byte-unchanged. NodeRecord/OpStoreRow shapes mirror across bindings.
byte_freeze_verified: zero-line diff vs baseline 1c6b83b on the three recovery suites (src/python/tests/test_no_recovery_surface.py, src/ts/tests/no-recovery-surface.test.ts, src/rust/crates/fathomdb/tests/no_recovery_surface.rs); all three green (Python suite passes; cargo test -p fathomdb --test no_recovery_surface ok; TS no-recovery-surface.test.ts passes).
additional_changes_made_in_scope: Anchor drift: ReaderRequest enum at lib.rs:375 (matched), ReaderResponse alias :423 (already Vec<SearchHit>, NOT Vec<String> — confirmed banner correction), search_inner :2067 (contract said 2122-2135 for the dispatch block, matched), read_search_in_tx :3648 / DEFERRED :3657 (matched). canonical_nodes G2 projection mirrors the :4170 ProjectionJob columns + logical_id.

Contract reference to TS `binding.d.ts` is stale — the TS native contract lives in `src/ts/src/binding.ts` (NativeEngine/NativeModule interfaces); updated there. PyO3 read fns are #[pyfunction]s (not PyEngine methods), mirroring admin_configure.
get_many partiality decision: partial + order-preserved, not-found = None slot (NOT all-or-nothing); read.get delegates to read_get_many.
not-found = None/null decision: normal absence, NO NotFound exception class (reserved-gap Slice 31).
read.mutations implemented as an alias surface over the same op-store read-back as read.collection (both are distinct governed allowlist verbs).

Reserved-gap candidates flagged (NOT built): 31 (typed NotFound class identical in both bindings), 32 (read.mutations cursor/limit hardening under ~1M load), 33 (CLI op-store read-back), 27 (Rust-facade allowlist pin, Q5=BIND-RUST).
Did NOT mint AC-075/AC-076 or new REQ/NEED ids (acceptance.md locked); did NOT add read.list/neighbors/history; did NOT add raw-SQL/filter-DSL; no release/CI/version actions.
Reverted an incidental `npm install` bump to src/ts/package-lock.json (version 0.6.1->0.7.2) to keep the slice scoped.
